title: Mapping Australia's Power System: Where the Transition Is Taking Shape
summary: A spatial view of Australia's electricity transition, connecting national generation trends with the geography of renewable capacity.
key_takeaway: Australia's transition is a physical build-out: generation resources, geography and grid connections shape whether renewable projects can contribute to a reliable low-emissions system.

inline_figures.caption: A facility-level renewable-capacity snapshot makes the geographic concentration of development visible.
The solar and wind mix in the source dataset varies by state, reinforcing that a national transition must be planned region by region.
